edited.speaker: Lisa
edited.text: We were called to meet Aliciet in the Brass District, a fancy part of town. We learned the East Mwangi Mining Company may also have to turn across slaves. If we could prove that, that would be awesome. And Darkwing is a mythical figure — whispered in dark shadows like Stephen and Longby Jekyll.
edited.sourceText: Okay, we were called to go meet Alicia in the brass blocks, which is a fancy part of town. We learned that the Deeslaw Mining Company may also have to turn across slaves. If we could prove that, that would be awesome. And then Darkwing is a mythical... whispered in Dark Shadows like Stephen and Longby Jekyll.

edited.speaker: Pablo
edited.text: We're all caught up. Supposedly First Count. We just finished our conversation with Aliciet, but you haven't left her office yet. After she explains everything and stands to escort you out, you hear a polite knock on her door. She says, come in. The same servant who helped you up the elevator enters, politely nods to each of you, hands her a note addressed to Dr. Cordova, and leaves. She reads the note. Roll a Perception check, everyone, as she's reading it.

Roll a perception test, everyone, as she's reading the note. And...

edited.speaker: Pablo
edited.text: What's your Perception, Brian? He rolled a 16 — a 5 on the die plus 11. You all notice a faint look of concern on her face as she reads the note. It fades quickly; she has a very good poker face — being a lawyer makes sense. She turns to you and says: I have troubling news. Black Heart Corsairs have been spotted entering the city a few hours ago. We're only just getting reports on it now. They usually hail from the west coast — honestly, he might know.

edited.speaker: Pablo
edited.text: Aliciet says the Black Heart Corsairs were seen entering the city. As she reads on, double-checking what she's saying, you recognize the name — they're a pirate war band, mercenaries who specialize in transporting slaves up and down the west coast of the Mwangi Expanse. A ruthless band of elves and drow who specialize in slavery and trapping exotic animals; the Mwangi Expanse has a rich assortment of both. Mr. Andy adds their pirate custom: chop the left ear if male, right ear if female. A small party seems to be heading toward the Bwamandu district, led by Morley Finch — and you see a look of contempt on her face she couldn't hide this time. A larger group, with the rest of the forces, is heading north in the general direction of the Brownbrook Barracks.
edited.sourceText: Okay, so she says that the Black Ark Corsairs were seen entering into the city. Somebody can call them mercenaries who specialize in transporting slaves up and down the west coast of the Mwangi Expanse. they're a ruthless band of elves and dark elves who specialize specifically in slavery and also trapping exotic animals where naturally the Mon die Expanse has a rich assortment of both Although, that pirate theme so far... ...that's heard from Dave first. Chop their left ear. Cut their left ear if they're male. Right ear if they're female. Anyways, it's all good. So... So a small party seems to be heading in the direction of the Wamandu district, headed by Morley Finch. And you can see this look of contempt in her. She couldn't even hide it this time. Morley Finch. And then a larger, with the rest of the group, heading north. She pauses. She pauses. in the general direction

edited.speaker: Pablo
edited.text: No, the other way around. Morley Finch and a small group of pirates are in the city and made camp in the Bwamandu district, where the Charau-ka live. The rest of the forces went north to Brownbrook Barracks.
